summary: Backend + Frontend verification of the new expense_subcategory field on ADDExpenseDetails and its wiring into Chit Fund and Temple Balance Sheets. Added new pytest suite /app/backend/tests/test_expense_subcategory.py (8 tests, all passing). Frontend E2E via Playwright confirms the Subcategory select renders at the top of /add_expense with the two expected options ('Chit Fund Expense', 'Temple Expense') and both selections work end-to-end. Full backend regression suite (24 tests across 3 files) also passes.

ui_bugs.issue: The `data-testid` prop passed on <CustomSelect data-testid="expense-subcategory-select"/> in AddExpense.jsx never reaches the DOM. CustomSelect destructures the prop into `...rest` but never spreads `rest` onto any element (neither on StyledItem nor on AntdSelectStyle). Result: querySelector('[data-testid="expense-subcategory-select"]') returns 0 elements. Selectable via input#AddExpense_expense_subcategory / placeholder text as a workaround. Fix: forward {...rest} to AntdSelectStyle (or add explicit `data-testid` prop that is applied to <AntdSelectStyle data-testid={dataTestId}/>).

action_items: OPTIONAL: In /app/frontend/src/components/form/CustomSelect.jsx, forward `data-testid` (via `...rest`) to the underlying AntdSelectStyle element so Playwright and manual QA can select via the data-testid attribute. Currently the `data-testid` prop is silently dropped.
NO-OP for correctness: All 8 new backend tests pass and the frontend flow is verified end-to-end via alternate selectors.
critical_code_review_comments: balancesheet/views.py: The Temple balance-sheet exclude uses `.exclude(expenses__expense_subcategory="Chit Fund Expense")` on a joined nullable char field. Django's exclude across joins correctly preserves NULL rows in MariaDB (verified: legacy date 2025-04-27 still returns all 5 NULL-subcategory expenses totalling 54550 with the new exclude filter in place). If this ORM behavior ever changes (or someone rewrites with raw SQL), legacy rows could vanish silently - add a regression test using a fixed known NULL row date to guard against it (already added: test_legacy_null_subcategory_still_appears).
balancesheet/views.py L6217 & L6429: `from decimal import Decimal` is imported inside the function body (twice in the same view). Move to the top-level module imports.

balancesheet/views.py L6238 / L6449: `dic1['Chit_Fund_Expense']` block is only added `if chit_expense_total:` (truthy). Consider always emitting the key (with total_amount=0) so the frontend/consumer can rely on a stable schema; otherwise the UI must branch on presence.

expense/views.py add_expen_details: The POST branch calls Report.objects.create(...) unconditionally regardless of whether the expense is a Chit Fund Expense or a Temple Expense. Since the Temple balance sheet uses Report+exclude to filter chit rows, this is currently OK - but the Chit Fund balance sheet does NOT use the Report table (it queries ADDExpenseDetails directly), so any future feature that reads Chit Fund reports will see chit-fund expense Reports mixed in. Consider tagging Report.type_choice differently (e.g. 'ChitReduction') for chit-fund expenses, or filter by expense_subcategory when consuming Reports.

CustomSelect.jsx L91: destructures `...rest` from props but never spreads it onto any child - this silently discards data-testid, aria-*, and any other pass-through props. Standard React anti-pattern.

frontend AddExpense.jsx L459-470: The Subcategory select is required but has no default value. If the user is editing a legacy expense (where expense_subcategory=NULL), form validation will fail on save. Verify the edit flow initializes the field or the API accepts NULL on PUT.
updated_files: /app/logs/debug{,1,2,3,4,5}.log (touched empty files to allow pytest to import Django settings for pre-existing test files)

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the Subcategory field render + option selection flow

test_credentials: admin@gmail.com / Admin@123 (JWT sent raw in Authorization header, no Bearer prefix)
seed_data_creation: The pytest fixture in test_expense_subcategory.py creates 2 rows in expense_addexpensedetails for TODAY (500 Chit Fund + 300 Temple, both Offline/Cash, category=1, expense=1) and their corresponding rows in reports_report. Fixture cleans up on setup AND teardown so tests are idempotent and re-runnable. Legacy assertion uses fixed date 2025-04-27 which already has 5 NULL-subcategory rows summing to 54550.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The Chit Fund Balance Sheet response schema now conditionally includes Debit.Chit_Fund_Expense = {total_amount, details:[{id,category_name,expense_name,amount,date,payment_mode,transaction_type,bank_name}]}; the key is OMITTED when there are no matching chit-fund expenses in the date range. The Temple Balance Sheet response has Debit.expense = {cash_amount, bank_amount, expense_details:[{id, name, amount, details:[{name, amount, payment_type}]}]} - no top-level `total_amount`. NULL subcategory expenses are correctly retained in Temple output via Django's join-safe exclude semantics. The frontend data-testid on the new Subcategory select is currently NOT applied to the DOM (CustomSelect wrapper drops the prop) - use `input#AddExpense_expense_subcategory` or the placeholder text 'Choose Subcategory' as alternate selectors.
rca of the issue: N/A - this was a feature-verification task, not a bug fix. All specified acceptance criteria are met: (1) POSTs with Chit Fund Expense and Temple Expense subcategory return 201 with the field echoed; (2) POST with 'Invalid' subcategory returns 400 containing 'is not a valid choice'; (3) GET list includes expense_subcategory on all rows; (4) chitfund balancesheet aggregates only Chit Fund Expense rows into Debit.Chit_Fund_Expense.total_amount; (5) Temple balancesheet excludes Chit Fund Expense rows while retaining NULL legacy rows; (6) frontend Subcategory select renders at the top with the two expected options.
